The mezze boxes for our office party from this caterer in Sin el Fil contained tabbouleh with wilted parsley swimming in cloudy juice. Three colleagues reported cramps after lunch. The hummus in two boxes had a fermented smell. We paid 22 dollars per box for thirty staff and the sales rep blamed our refrigerator.

I bought herbal slimming tea from this wellness shop in Hamra and the sealed bags inside smelled moldy when opened. After one cup I had heart palpitations and dizziness. The package claimed Ministry approval but had no registration code. The shop assistant said reactions mean the tea is working.

I got a cheese fatayer from a stall in the Bourj Hammoud parking garage food row and bit into cold raw dough in the center. The cheese filling tasted sour. I felt stomach pain during my commute to Jounieh. The baker stacked pastries in open cardboard boxes near car exhaust fumes.

The mixed grill I ordered at this restaurant in Baalbek arrived with kibbeh that was raw inside and leaking pink fluid. The waiter insisted it is supposed to be soft but I have eaten kibbeh nayeh and this was labeled as grilled kibbeh on the menu. I paid 175000 LBP and left hungry fearing illness.

I found packs of sliced turkey in this supermarket with a sell-by date next week but the slices were grey and slimy at the edges. The cold cuts section smelled like rotten eggs when I opened the door. An employee said the fridge thermostat was fixed yesterday but products were not discarded.

I bought a whole chicken from this poultry shop in Chouf and noticed the skin had greenish patches under the wing joints. During cooking the kitchen filled with a chemical odor. Nobody ate the meal and we ordered takeaway instead. The shop owner said the color comes from feed and refused a refund.

The insulin pen I collected was stored in a plastic bag inside a hot car the delivery boy left running outside this pharmacy. The pharmacist said delivery is outsourced and they cannot control transport. The pen felt warm to touch and the leaflet says never expose to heat above 30 degrees.

I purchased zaatar manoushe from this oven in Dbayeh and the zaatar tasted bitter with a chemical note like old oil. The dough had dark specks that might be mold. My son vomited after eating half. The baker said the oven is wood fired and flavor varies daily without addressing the visible specks.

We rented sunbeds at this beach in Anfeh and bought fried calamari that tasted ammonia-strong and rubbery. My husband had diarrhea after lunch. The kitchen is a metal shed with no visible ventilation behind the bar. We spent 90 dollars between entry food and drinks for our family of four.

The smoothie I ordered at this hotel pool bar used fruit that tasted fermented and the cup had dried residue inside before filling. I felt bloated and nauseous within an hour. The bartender rinsed the cup quickly when I pointed it out but did not remake the drink. I paid 18 dollars for a small smoothie.

Room service sent a seafood risotto to my suite at this hotel in Downtown Beirut and the mussels were open and empty with a foul odor. I ate two bites before stopping and called the doctor service. They charged 380000 LBP and removed the dish charge only after I threatened to post photos online.

The grilled liver sandwich from a cart near Charles Helou station had liver pieces still bleeding when I bit into it. I felt feverish that night and missed work. The vendor cooked on a portable grill with charcoal but rushed orders by undercooking. He had no water for handwashing visible on the cart.

I bought a tuna wrap from this petrol station shop and the tortilla was slimy with sour tuna smell inside. The wrapper label had no production time. I ate half during a road trip to Zahle and had to stop twice for bathroom emergencies. The manager said turnover is low so items stay longer.

Our wedding tasting with this caterer included kibbeh that smelled off and raw meat mix stored in a warm hallway outside the kitchen. I became ill that evening with vomiting. We cancelled the contract but lost a 500 dollar deposit. The owner said illness was unrelated despite only eating their samples that day.

I purchased imported honey from this shop and the jar had foam and a yeasty smell suggesting fermentation. The label had no batch number. After spreading it on bread I felt stomach discomfort. The owner said natural honey ferments in heat and refused return because I opened the jar.

I bought a ready chicken shawarma plate from a kiosk in the Dora highway rest stop and found a piece of plastic glove in the meat. I photographed it and reported to the cashier who said it must have come from my bag. I had not opened the container until sitting at the table.

The open salad bar at this restaurant in Ashrafieh had no sneeze guard and customers leaned directly over lettuce with their hands. I saw a child grab cherry tomatoes and return them to the bowl. Staff did not refresh the display during the two hours I dined. The manager said the guard broke and parts are on order.

I noticed rat traps in the pet food aisle of this supermarket but also saw droppings near bulk rice sacks in the storeroom visible through an open door. Staff carried boxes from that room directly to shelf without gloves. I reported it to the manager who closed the door and said pest control comes monthly.

The fishmonger at this shop in Saida used one knife for gutting fish and slicing fillets for customers without sanitizing between tasks. Guts remained on the cutting board while he served my sea bass order. The ice bed was yellowed and smelled. I canceled my purchase after watching the process.

This pharmacy keeps returned medications on a shelf behind the counter mixed with new stock before disposal. I saw opened boxes with missing blister strips sitting open during my wait. The pharmacist handled my prescription after sorting those returns without gloves. I worry about cross contamination of dispensed drugs.

Workers at this manoushe bakery in Tripoli roll dough on a table covered in cigarette ash from breaks taken beside the oven. Ash fell onto dough balls repeatedly and staff brushed it off with bare hands. The oven chimney vents black smoke onto the preparation area. I canceled my order after watching from the counter.

The outdoor toilets at this beach club near Tyre had feces smeared on the door handle and no cleaner on duty Sunday afternoon. Guests walked barefoot from bathrooms across the dining deck. I reported it twice and staff handed me air freshener instead of closing facilities for cleaning.

The gym sauna at this sports club pool area had towels reused from a open bin without visible laundry tags. Mold grew on shower curtains and the drain in the men's locker room was clogged. Membership costs 800 dollars yearly and hygiene has declined over six months with no management response to complaints.

Housekeeping at this hotel in Chtoura left used dental floss on the bathroom counter and did not replace drinking glasses wrapped in dirty tissue. I requested recleaning and the attendant wiped surfaces with the same cloth used on the toilet seat. I documented photos for a group booking of twelve rooms.

The shawarma cart near Sassine Square had meat shavings collected from the rotisserie floor tray back onto the spit when the vendor thought no one was watching. Oil dripping pan sat unwashed all day. I confronted him and he denied it though I recorded a short video.

The bathroom at this Coop station in Zahle had no running water for handwashing and the soap dispenser was empty for weeks according to the attendant. Yet the shop sells manakish and pastries at the counter. I reported it to the regional office number posted on the wall and received no callback.

During our engagement hall tasting the catering staff wore no gloves while shaping raw kibbeh and then handled bread baskets for the table. The kitchen had open windows without screens and flies landed on food trays. We hired a different caterer but want authorities to inspect this kitchen.

The beauty shop facial room had towels with visible stains and tools soaked in a cloudy jar of liquid that smelled old. The aesthetician double dipped into product jars using the same spatula on my face and the display shelf. I developed a rash the next day and the owner blamed my skin sensitivity.

The shared food alley in this market in Baalbek had sewage water flowing across the walkway near kebab grills. Vendors stepped over the stream and continued cooking without washing boots or tools. Health inspectors should visit before the tourist season peaks.

I saw the dishwasher at this restaurant in Gemmayze return glasses to the bar with lipstick stains and food residue still visible. The bartender wiped them with a bar rag and served drinks immediately. I asked for a clean glass and the waiter rolled his eyes saying it is sterile enough.

The cheese counter at this hypermarket stacks unpacked halloumi directly on metal shelves without wrap while shoppers touch samples with bare hands. Staff cut samples using the same knife without cleaning between cheeses. I saw a child sneeze near the display and no product was removed afterward.

This butcher in Metn stores lamb heads unwrapped on hooks above the customer counter dripping onto packaged mince below. Flies gather on the heads during afternoon hours. The owner says traditional display requires hanging heads but customers stand inches from dripping fat.

The pharmacy waiting area had a cat sleeping on boxes of diapers sold to customers. Staff petted the cat then handled prescription bags without hand hygiene. I asked them to remove the cat and the pharmacist said it catches mice so they tolerate it.

The display fridge at this patisserie in Achrafieh had condensation dripping onto uncovered eclairs below the cooling fan. Water mixed with cream on several pastries. Staff wiped the shelf with a dirty cloth and returned pastries to sale. I pointed it out and the cashier removed only one eclair.

Lifeguards at this beach in Jbeil ate lunch on the rescue tower chair then entered the water without showering. Food wrappers floated near the swimming zone at high tide. Management ignored my written comment card about separating lifeguard breaks from active duty.

The kids pool at this resort had a brown scum line at the water edge and bandages visible under the surface near the steps. Parents complained at the desk and staff added chlorine powder while children were still swimming. No temporary closure occurred during treatment.

The spa at this mountain hotel reused disposable slippers collected from checkout rooms without sanitizing. I found someone else's hair in the robe pocket. The spa manager said laundry is outsourced and mistakes happen. The treatment costs 90 dollars per session.

The price board at this supermarket listed President butter at 38000 LBP but checkout charged 47000 LBP. I bought three packs and was overcharged 27000 LBP total. The supervisor blamed a central pricing update that did not reach shelves. This is the third time in two months at the same branch.

This beach parking attendant charged 10000 LBP though the sign says 5000 LBP and claimed the rate doubled for holidays without updating the sign. He blocked our car until we paid. We have a photo of the sign dated that morning with the lower price.

The restaurant added a cover charge of 15000 LBP per person not listed on the menu or announced at entry. Our party of six paid 90000 LBP extra without explanation on the itemized bill. The owner said live music nights always include cover though no musician performed that evening.

The pump at this Wardieh station reset incorrectly and charged me for 45 liters when my tank holds 40 maximum. The attendant insisted the meter is accurate and refused to check CCTV. My fuel gauge showed the tank was not full after the fill.

The catering quote listed dessert at 3 dollars per person but the invoice added a cake cutting fee of 150 dollars never mentioned before. Our event contract attachment did not include that line. The finance office said fees are standard though we never received the fee schedule.

I bought a laptop bag marked 25 dollars at this electronics kiosk in City Mall but paid 39 dollars at the register. The seller said the tag was for an older model though the barcode matched. The mall management office said tenant pricing is not their responsibility.

I bought lamb chops weighed at 1.1 kg on the butcher receipt but at home the scale showed 0.85 kg. The butcher charged 290000 LBP at the higher weight. When I returned he claimed moisture loss during transport reduces weight at home which makes no sense for sealed meat.

This pharmacy sold contact lens solution at double the manufacturer suggested price printed on the box side panel. The receipt showed a markup of 90000 LBP above the listed MSRP equivalent. The pharmacist said import costs justify any price without showing documentation.

The bakery charged 80000 LBP for a standard manoushe tray quoted at 55000 LBP on the phone order. Delivery added 25000 LBP though the shop is two streets away. The owner said phone prices are outdated and hung up when I asked for a written price list.

The aqua park sold combo tickets on the website for 20 dollars but at the gate demanded 28 dollars citing maintenance fees. The website footer had no maintenance fee notice. My children were disappointed waiting in line while I argued with the cashier.

A fruit vendor at Souk el Tayeb charged 40000 LBP for peaches while the chalkboard listed 25000 LBP per kilo. He weighed with a handheld scale I could not read and refused to show the weight number. I paid because the market was closing but kept photos of the board.

This Medco station charged premium fuel prices on the pump labeled regular 95 octane. My car knocked on the drive home suggesting lower octane fuel. The manager said tanks were mixed during delivery and customers should have noticed the price difference.

The restaurant receipt showed a 10 percent municipality tax not mentioned anywhere in the dining room. I asked the waiter and he said it is new but could not show the official notice. The tax added 18000 LBP to a modest dinner for two.

We agreed on 18 dollars per head for buffet catering and received an invoice with a service staff fee of 250 dollars for two servers we never requested. The contract signature page did not mention staffing charges. The company owner said servers are mandatory for events over thirty guests.

The toy store advertised buy two get one free on building blocks but at checkout the cashier said the deal excludes the brand I selected though the poster shows that brand logo. I paid full price for three boxes totaling 72 dollars. The manager was unavailable.

The corner grocer charges card payments in dollars at 92000 LBP per dollar when the bank rate was 89500 that day. He said dollar prices are fixed on the shelf and LBP conversion is his choice. I was overcharged on a 12 dollar basket by roughly 30000 LBP.

This fast food restaurant in Dora operates with a faded health permit behind the counter that staff could not read when I asked the expiry date. The manager said renewal is in progress for six months. They serve hundreds of meals daily to students from nearby schools.

I bought chocolate spread imported from Europe with no Arabic ingredient list and no Lebanese importer address on the jar. The supermarket buyer said products arrive through a free zone and labels are optional. Ministry rules require Arabic consumer information.

The butcher advertises halal meat but displays no halal certification from any recognized authority. I asked for the certificate and received a WhatsApp image of an unreadable foreign document. Many Muslim families shop here trusting the halal sign painted on the window.

I watched a pharmacy intern sell morning after pills without pharmacist supervision or prescription check at this branch in Hamra. The intern said the pharmacist was at lunch for two hours. Controlled sales require licensed oversight under Lebanese pharmacy law.

This bakery sells packaged kaak with a label showing a fictional company address in Beirut that does not exist on maps. The Ministry registration number printed on the bag does not appear in public databases I checked. I reported it because children eat these snacks daily.

The beach resort jet ski rental operates with no visible insurance certificate or operator license posted near the rental desk. Riders sign a waiver only. My friend witnessed a minor collision and staff could not provide insurance details when asked.

The diving board at this public pool was reopened after repair with no engineering inspection certificate displayed as required for public facilities. The pool manager said verbal approval from a contractor is sufficient. Families with children use the board daily.

The popcorn cart outside a cinema in Beirut sells flavored popcorn with no vendor health permit on the cart and no ingredient list for allergy sufferers. The operator said cinema management authorizes him verbally. Teenagers buy large bags every evening.

The catering kitchen operates from a residential apartment with no commercial zoning permit displayed when I picked up samples. The owner said home kitchens are legal for catering under a certain size but could show no exemption letter from the municipality.

The host at this restaurant seated us at a wobbly table near the kitchen trash door and refused to move us despite empty tables by the window. When we asked again the host said we should have reserved a better table. We spent 200000 LBP and left before dessert.

The butcher whistled and joked with friends while I waited with a numbered ticket for twenty minutes. When my number was called he had already sold my cut to someone else and told me to take a different piece or wait again. I needed the specific cut for a family recipe.

The pharmacy refused to order a medication for my father saying it is not profitable for them despite the prescription being standard. They offered a more expensive alternative without explaining medical equivalence. I felt pressured while my father waited in the car in pain.

I placed a cake order two weeks ahead for my daughter's graduation and the bakery lost the order completely on pickup day. The owner offered a plain sponge cake with no decoration as replacement. We had forty guests arriving and had to buy desserts from another shop in panic.

The beach club security guard spoke rudely to my wife about her beach dress length though other guests wore similar clothing. He threatened to remove us without manager consultation. We felt targeted and left after paying full entry. Management never responded to my email.

The swim coach at this club pool yelled at my son for using the wrong lane though no lane markers were visible that day. Other parents complained about the same coach's behavior. Membership fees are high and children should not be humiliated publicly.

The hotel valet scratched my car door and denied responsibility saying the scratch was pre-existing. I had photos from before drop-off showing a clean door. The valet supervisor refused to file an incident report and told me to contact my insurance.

The crepe vendor at the weekend market ignored my order for fifteen minutes while serving friends who arrived later. When I complained he said tourists should be patient. I paid 25000 LBP and received a burnt crepe folded carelessly without apology.

The station attendant insisted I pay cash when my card worked fine at the same pump last week. He said the machine is broken but I saw him process cards for other customers afterward. I feel I was discriminated against because I questioned the meter reading.

The catering company changed the menu items without notice at our company gala and served chicken instead of promised fish for VIP tables. The event manager shrugged and said supply issues are normal. Our CEO was embarrassed in front of partners.

The phone shop technician kept my device for two weeks beyond the promised repair date without updates. When I called daily staff hung up or said the technician is busy. I needed the phone for work and lost freelance income.

The clerk at this service shop mocked my accent when I asked for help in Arabic mixed with English. She spoke loudly to a coworker implying I was wasting her time. I left without buying though I needed urgent printing for a government appointment.

I used a pharmacy in Bourj Hammoud after sunset where I paid 640000 LBP to get wound spray. The experience was unacceptable since the hotline transferred me repeatedly without ownership and nobody took responsibility. When I raised the issue the kitchen captain told me to leave quickly. I felt unsafe and financially exploited in the same moment. I need a surprise inspection and a written penalty. I was charged in LBP and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

I stopped at a bakery in Sin el Fil after sunset and spent 640000 LBP on spinach fatayer. The transaction felt dishonest since promo sticker stayed on expired deal and no correction was made. The cashier heard me out then raised his voice in front of customers. I returned home upset because trust in basic consumer rights keeps dropping. I called the hotline yet no one returned my report. I was charged in LBP and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

I stopped at a bakery in Hamra on a holiday evening and spent 820000 LBP on manoushe zaatar. Instead of helping me they allowed chaos as they closed the desk before posted hours in front of everyone. When I raised the issue the counter worker claimed the system cannot be changed. I do not want another customer to face this avoidable harm. I kept photos and timestamped videos as proof. I was charged in LBP and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

I entered a pharmacy in Sin el Fil while schools were ending and handed over 1050000 LBP for nasal rinse. The product looked fresh outside yet inside I found grey spots under the sauce and felt bloating and severe discomfort. My complaint reached the security guard but he kept me waiting without answer. I felt unsafe and financially exploited in the same moment. I wrote down names because accountability keeps disappearing. I was charged in LBP and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

I challenged the amount after noticing that display price excluded mandatory service fee before payment completed. I wrote down names because accountability keeps disappearing. I was charged in LBP and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

I requested license proof and they replied that temporary approval is enough while staying fully operational. My complaint reached the service desk agent but he said I should accept it or go elsewhere. I do not want another customer to face this avoidable harm. I need a surprise inspection and a written penalty. I was charged in LBP and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

The pricing was deceptive because menu board showed one rate then receipt showed more and the final bill jumped unfairly. My complaint reached the security guard but he raised his voice in front of customers. I do not want another customer to face this avoidable harm. I expect a refund and corrective action that can be verified. I was charged in LBP and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

The service desk agent heard me out then claimed the system cannot be changed. I do not want another customer to face this avoidable harm. I left with stress and wasted transport costs. I was charged in LBP and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

I reported it to the security guard who blamed another shift. I do not want another customer to face this avoidable harm. I called the hotline yet no one returned my report. I was charged in LBP and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

I returned home upset because trust in basic consumer rights keeps dropping. I am asking for immediate enforcement before more families are harmed. I was charged in LBP and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

I returned home upset because trust in basic consumer rights keeps dropping. I left with stress and wasted transport costs. I was charged in LBP and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

I was charged in LBP and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

I was charged in LBP and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

My complaint reached the owner representative but he kept me waiting without answer. I am sharing this incident so inspection teams can verify the branch quickly. I am asking for immediate enforcement before more families are harmed. I was charged in LBP and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

I was charged in USD and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

I expect transparent action because this pattern is repeating across neighborhoods. I expect a refund and corrective action that can be verified. I was charged in LBP and no clear written policy was provided at that moment.

This petrol station convenience store sells vape products with no Arabic health warnings and no separate retail license beyond the fuel permit on the wall. Imported pods have unknown origins. The cashier said tobacco rules do not apply to vapes.

Imported protein bars at this gym shop have no Arabic ingredient list and no importer address on the wrapper. The seller said fitness products are exempt though they are sold to the general public. Ministry labeling rules should still apply.

The restaurant expanded a tent structure on the sidewalk in Sassine without a municipal permit for public space occupation. Pedestrians must walk in the street to pass. I asked the manager for the permit file and he said it is being processed for two months.

Baby formula tins at this supermarket have stickers covering original expiry dates with new dates pasted on top. The importer name is missing from the Arabic label. I reported it to the manager who removed one tin but left others on display.

Our waiter at this restaurant in Monot forgot our appetizer and blamed the kitchen when we asked after forty minutes. The manager never appeared though the dining room was quiet. We received cold mains and no discount on the 280000 LBP bill.

I waited thirty-five minutes at the butcher counter while the employee served friends in the back room. When my turn came he rushed the cut and gave me the wrong thickness twice. He refused to fix it saying he was closing soon.

The pharmacist snapped at me when I asked about mixing two prescriptions and said she does not have time for lessons. She turned to gossip with a visitor behind the counter. I am elderly and needed clear guidance about timing doses.

The clothing store refused a tag-on return within seven days citing a hidden sale policy not printed on the receipt. The manager hid in the back and staff said come back next month. I paid 55 dollars for a shirt that did not fit.

The clerk at this copy center in Hadath hung up twice when I called about a misprinted wedding invitation order. In person she offered only a 10 percent discount on reprints though the error was theirs. We had 200 guests arriving in three days.

The host at this restaurant in Ashrafieh sat us at a table next to the bathroom door despite empty window tables. When we asked to move he said reservations are required for better seats though we walked in at an off-peak hour. We left before ordering mains.

The supermarket cashier closed her lane when she saw my full cart and said she was on break without calling another cashier. I waited fifteen minutes while other lanes served smaller baskets. The supervisor watched from the office and did not intervene.

The butcher mocked my request for lean cuts saying city people do not understand meat. He slammed the meat on the scale and overcharged by guessing weight when I asked to see the display. I felt humiliated in front of other customers.

The station attendant insisted on cash only for me though the card machine worked for others. I believe I was targeted after disputing a meter reading last week. I had to find an ATM while my children waited in the car.

Security at this supermarket accused my mother of shoplifting at self-checkout and searched her bag publicly finding nothing. The manager offered no apology saying the guard did his job. She refuses to return to the only large store near her home.

I called this restaurant three times to modify a reservation for twelve guests and each staff member gave a different answer. On the day they seated us at separate tables without explanation. The celebration was disrupted and the manager blamed our late arrival though we were on time.

The supermarket express lane cashier chatted on her phone for two minutes while I stood with a crying baby and a full basket. She scanned items roughly damaging a loaf of bread. The supervisor line was empty and nobody helped.

The neighborhood grocer refused to sell me a single item saying card minimum is 200000 LBP though I offered to pay the difference in cash. He waved me away while serving another customer buying one soda with cash. I felt discriminated against.

The butcher closed the shop ten minutes early and rushed me out while my order was half prepared. He kept partial payment and said come tomorrow though I needed the meat for guests tonight. He locked the door while I was still at the counter.

The restaurant manager told us to leave when we asked about allergen information for a nut allergy sufferer at our table. He said the kitchen is too busy for special questions. We had already ordered drinks and felt unsafe staying.

The hotel breakfast buffet scrambled eggs sat in a chafing dish with crust forming for over ninety minutes. I ate a portion and had stomach cramps before my meeting in Downtown Beirut. The restaurant manager offered coffee but no medical assistance. The buffet charge was 38 dollars per person on my room bill.

I bought a chicken shawarma from a cart near AUB and the meat looked dried and dark on the rotisserie. Two hours later I had diarrhea and dehydration. The vendor stored garlic sauce open in the sun and handled cash then sliced meat without gloves. No permit was posted on the cart.

I purchased a tuna sandwich from the fridge at this petrol station on the Damour highway and the bread was slimy with sour tuna smell. I ate half during a road trip and had to stop twice for bathroom emergencies. The attendant said turnover is low so sandwiches stay longer than they should.

Our office lunch catering arrived with rice lukewarm and clumped suggesting it sat in the van from Dahieh to Dora too long. Three colleagues vomited that afternoon. We paid 950000 LBP for fifteen meals and the caterer blamed traffic without accepting responsibility.

I bought imported peanut butter from this wellness shop and found mold under the lid when I opened it at home. The shopkeeper said jar goods sometimes arrive that way and refused return. I paid 65000 LBP and worry other jars on the shelf are contaminated.

I got a ready chicken plate from a kiosk in Bourj Hammoud market and bit into cold raw dough in a fatayer piece. The cheese filling tasted sour and I felt stomach pain on my commute. The seller stacked pastries in open boxes near car exhaust.

The mixed grill at this restaurant in Baalbek arrived with kibbeh raw in the center leaking pink fluid. The waiter insisted softness is normal but the menu listed grilled kibbeh not raw. I paid 175000 LBP and left hungry fearing illness after my companion vomited in the parking lot.

I found sliced turkey in this supermarket with sell-by date next week but grey slimy edges and rotten egg smell in the cold cuts section. An employee said the fridge thermostat was fixed yesterday without discarding affected stock. I reported it for elderly shoppers who may not notice.

I bought a whole chicken from this poultry shop in Chouf with greenish patches under the wing joints. Cooking released a chemical odor and nobody ate the meal. The owner said feed color causes green skin and refused refund for the 125000 LBP purchase.

I purchased zaatar manoushe from this oven in Dbayeh and the zaatar tasted bitter with chemical notes like rancid oil. The dough had dark specks that might be mold. My son vomited after eating half. The baker blamed wood oven flavor without inspecting the batch.

We bought fried calamari at this beach in Anfeh that tasted ammonia-strong and rubbery. My husband had diarrhea after lunch. The kitchen is a metal shed with no ventilation behind the bar. We spent 90 dollars between entry food and drinks for our family.

The smoothie at this hotel pool bar used fermented fruit and the cup had dried residue inside before filling. I felt bloated and nauseous within an hour. The bartender rinsed quickly when I pointed it out but did not remake the drink. I paid 18 dollars.

Room service seafood risotto at this Downtown hotel had open mussels with foul odor. I ate two bites before stopping and called the doctor service. They charged 380000 LBP and removed the dish charge only after I threatened to post photos.

I bought a tuna wrap from this petrol station and the tortilla was slimy with sour smell inside. The label had no production time. I ate half on a road trip to Zahle and had bathroom emergencies twice. The manager said low turnover keeps items longer.

Our wedding tasting included kibbeh that smelled off and raw meat mix stored in a warm hallway. I became ill that evening with vomiting. We cancelled the contract but lost a 500 dollar deposit. The owner denied linkage despite only eating their samples.

I could see into the open kitchen at this restaurant in Byblos and watched staff handle raw chicken then plate fattoush without washing hands. Grease pooled near the walk-in fridge and flies landed on herbs. The waiter shrugged when I mentioned it saying the kitchen is always busy.

Locker rooms at this Kaslik club pool had mold on ceiling panels and wet towels in an open reuse bin. Shower heads were coated in scale and one drain backed up. Membership services gave no follow up after two weeks.

The dishwasher at this Gemmayze bar returned lipstick-stained glasses wiped with a bar rag only. I asked for a clean glass and the waiter rolled his eyes. Grease coated the exhaust hood above the open kitchen visible from my table.

The 5L sunflower oil shelf tag showed 185000 LBP but checkout charged 225000 LBP at this Nabatieh supermarket. The supervisor removed the tag while I stood there saying prices updated at noon. I photographed the tag five minutes earlier on my phone.

I ordered the fish sayadieh at this Tyre harbor restaurant on Friday and the fillet center remained translucent while the rice was hot. My husband and I both felt ill before midnight with matching symptoms. We paid 240000 LBP and the waiter argued fresh catch is softer than frozen fish without offering replacement.

I bought frozen berries from this Sin el Fil supermarket that had thawed and refrozen into a solid icy block. At home the berries were mushy and fermented smelling. The freezer unit clearly malfunctioned and customer service refused compensation saying frozen goods are sold as-is.

The salad bar at this Achrafieh restaurant had no sneeze guard and a child returned cherry tomatoes to the bowl with bare hands. Staff did not refresh the display during two hours I dined. The manager said the guard broke and replacement parts are on order since last month.

Rat traps sat in the pet aisle but droppings remained near bulk rice sacks visible through an open storeroom door at this hypermarket. Staff carried boxes from that room to shelves without gloves. The manager closed the door when I reported it without cleaning the aisle.

The fishmonger in Saida gutted fish and sliced fillets with one unwashed knife while guts stayed on the board during my order. The ice bed was yellowed and smelled strongly. I canceled purchase after watching the full process from the counter.

Cigarette ash from staff breaks fell onto dough balls at this Tripoli manoushe bakery beside the oven. Workers brushed ash off with bare hands and continued baking. Black smoke from the chimney vented onto the preparation table throughout the morning rush.

Feces smeared the beach club toilet door handle near Tyre and no cleaner worked Sunday afternoon despite 25 dollar entry fees. Guests walked barefoot from bathrooms across the dining deck. Management handed air freshener instead of closing facilities for proper cleaning.

Sauna towels at this Antelias sports club pool were reused from an open bin without laundry tags. Mold grew on shower curtains and the men's drain was clogged. An 800 dollar yearly membership should include basic locker room hygiene maintained daily.

Housekeeping in Chtoura left dental floss and used soap from prior guests and wiped the toilet seat with the same cloth used on drinking glasses. I requested recleaning for a group booking of twelve rooms and the supervisor argued the room was fine.

The Sassine shawarma vendor swept meat shavings from the floor tray back onto the spit when he thought nobody watched. The dripping oil pan sat unwashed all afternoon. I recorded video and reported to the municipality food hotline the same evening.

The Zahle Coop station bathroom had no running water for weeks per the attendant while manakish sell beside the open bathroom door. I reported to the regional office number on the wall and received no callback after ten days waiting.

The water park charged 20 dollars online but 28 dollars at the gate citing maintenance fees with no website notice. My children waited in line while I argued with the cashier who pointed to a laminated note inside the booth not visible from outside.

Souk el Tayeb peach vendor charged 40000 LBP while the chalkboard listed 25000 LBP per kilo. He used a handheld scale I could not read and refused to show the weight number. I kept photos of the board before paying under pressure at market close.

The birthday cake catalog showed 130000 LBP all-inclusive but pickup billing reached 180000 LBP with fondant fees I never selected. The manager added delivery charges though I collected at the counter myself. I have the catalog photo showing bundled pricing.

The butchery wall price board listed lamb chops at one rate but receipts charged premium cut prices per kilogram. The owner said board prices are wholesale only with no notice that retail customers pay different rates walking in from the street.

The fast food restaurant in Dora operates with a faded unreadable health permit behind the counter for over six months. Staff could not state the expiry date when asked. Hundreds of school students eat lunch here daily near the campus gate.

The waiter sighed and slammed my corrected dish on the table after bringing the wrong meal twice on a quiet Tuesday. We waited forty-five minutes between courses with four tables occupied. He claimed to be the manager when I asked for escalation.

The petrol attendant filled wrong fuel grade and blamed me for not specifying though I always use 95 octane at this Sin el Fil station. I waited twenty minutes for drainage after his error while he remained on his phone.

The patisserie display fridge dripped condensation onto uncovered eclairs below the cooling fan in Achrafieh. Water mixed with cream on several pastries and staff wiped the shelf with a dirty cloth before returning them to sale. I pointed it out and the cashier removed only one eclair while leaving the rest.

Engagement hall catering staff shaped raw kibbeh without gloves then handled bread baskets for our tasting table. Open kitchen windows had no screens and flies landed on appetizer trays throughout the visit. We hired another caterer but want this kitchen inspected before the next wedding season.

The beach club parking attendant charged 10000 LBP while the entrance sign listed 5000 LBP and claimed holiday rates without updating the board. He blocked our car until we paid. We photographed the sign that morning showing the lower price before the attendant covered part of it.

The restaurant added a 15000 LBP per person cover charge on live music night without menu notice and no musician performed that evening. Our party of six paid 90000 LBP extra on a 320000 LBP dinner. The owner said cover applies automatically on Fridays.

The catering final invoice added 250 dollars for service staff we never requested on a fifty-person buffet quoted at 15 dollars per head. The signed attachment listed food only with no staffing line items. The owner stopped replying after we paid the deposit.

The toy store refused buy-two-get-one-free on building blocks claiming the poster brand was excluded though the poster displayed that exact brand logo. I paid full price for three boxes totaling 72 dollars. The cashier said promotions are at manager discretion only.

The Chouf corner grocer converts shelf dollar prices at 92000 LBP per dollar when banks posted 89500 that day. I was overcharged roughly 30000 LBP on a twelve dollar basket. He said dollar shelf tags are fixed and lira conversion is his choice.

The pool locker rental sign said complimentary but reception charged 10 dollars per locker with no updated pricing in the lobby. The cashier said the old sign remains while a new fee applies since last month without any posted notice at the entrance.

The sushi restaurant in Beirut Marina has no readable food handling permit and staff could not produce a current certificate when asked. Neighboring restaurants display Ministry of Public Health permits at the entrance. They serve raw fish daily to tourists.

Chocolate spread jars here have English-only labels with no Arabic nutrition table or Lebanese importer contact on the shelf. The buyer said free zone shipments sell before labeling is completed. Ministry rules require Arabic consumer information at point of sale.

The hotel concierge promised a 6 AM taxi that never arrived causing a missed flight. The night manager denied the booking note with his signature existed and offered 50 dollars credit against a 200 dollar rebooking fee I paid myself.

The butcher refused standard fat trimming saying city customers do not understand meat and threw my order on the scale aggressively when I insisted. Other customers left after hearing the exchange. I have shopped there five years without prior conflict.

The hotel restaurant buffet displays a food handling permit that expired in 2024 near the dining room entrance. The hostess directed me to the faded frame when asked about renewal. International guests eat daily at this well-known property expecting current Ministry of Public Health certification visible.

The university street food cart near Haigazian has no municipality vendor permit posted and serves hundreds of students daily from 7 AM to midnight. The vendor said small carts need no license when I asked for his permit number during lunch rush.

Packaged mouneh jars at this bakery sell with homemade labels and no Ministry of Public Health registration number from a commercial storefront on a main Baabda road. The owner sells dozens weekly claiming cottage producers are exempt from facility registration.

The Bourj Hammoud market point sells imported phone chargers with no Arabic safety warnings and no Ministry type approval sticker on retail packaging. The owner said small shipments from the port skip registration to keep prices low for neighborhood buyers.

Our theater tickets were missed because the restaurant forgot our order entirely and blamed a busy Friday kitchen with half the tables empty. The manager offered nothing beyond saying delays happen. We waited thirty minutes after reminding the waiter twice.

The supermarket security guard publicly searched my elderly father accusing him of shoplifting at self-checkout and found nothing. The manager said the guard did his job and gave no apology. My father refuses to return to the only large store near Jal el Dib.

The delivery driver from this catering service threw bags on my doorstep and left before I could verify missing sauces and half the vegetarian meals. The restaurant said delivery is outsourced and not their responsibility for a 250 dollar order with guests waiting.

The pool manager refused entry to my nephew over a visible birthmark mistaken for a contagious skin condition in front of other guests at the entrance gate. I explained it is a lifelong birthmark and offered a doctor note. They still denied entry without apology for the public humiliation we experienced before leaving.
